title: Mobile Accessories, Docks, Peripherals, and Local Connectivity
description: A field team uses docks, styluses, headsets, keyboards, card readers, webcams, hotspots, biometric devices, and chargers that pair or connect but fail under changed ports, profiles, power, and accessibility needs. Learners cumulatively produce mobile accessory compatibility and connection matrix through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Match docks, port replicators, input devices, headsets, webcams, speakers, card readers, security keys, and specialty accessories to user tasks.
Trace connector, cable capability, port role, negotiated power, hub topology, bandwidth sharing, driver, permission, and selected-device state.
Distinguish discovery, pairing, trust, profile, codec or service, range, interference, battery, privacy, and reconnect behavior.
